``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Under the general rules for people in prison convicted of nonviolent crimes parole-release eligibility occurs at the 25 percent mark of their judicial maximum terms or 10 years whichever is less. For most people in prison convicted of violent offenses parole-release eligibility comes at the 50-percent milestone of their maximum terms or after 20 years whichever is less.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 559 people in prison past their parole consideration year. This group made up 5% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole consideration year are mostly notable proportions among Black, non-Hispanic and White, non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: In 2020, 83% of people in prison past their parole consideration year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for property (34%) and drugs (29%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison past their parole consideration year had original sentence lengths between 5 to 9.9 years representing 43% of those eligible for parole. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data This section will contain information related to missingness in the NCRP data. Keep?


# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 18%, changing from 21,067 in 2010 to 17,332 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series
## Demographics The demographics of people in prison are mostly notable proportions among Black, non-Hispanic and White, non-Hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners 2022 - Race and Ethnicity
## Criminal Offenses In 2020, 57% of people in prison were incarcerated for violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses reveals a more varied landscape, with most people incarcerated for property (19%) and drugs (17%) offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Sentence Lengths In 2020, most people in prison had original sentence lengths between 10 to 24.9 years representing 40%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases decreased 24%, dropping from 9,883 in 2010 to 7,463 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2020, 76% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 76% increase compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison are mostly notable proportions among Black, non-Hispanic and white, non-hispanic people. By gender, there were more males than females. Age-wise, most people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Time Served by Offense Between 2010 and 2020, there were shifts in the average time served by individuals for different offense types in Mississippi. The largest change was for robbery offenses, which increased by 55%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In Mississippi, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 2.9 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity In 2020, people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 519 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole.

## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
## Time Served ::: {.panel-tabset .custom-tabs} ### Race and Ethnicity ::: {.cell} ::: Black people faced the longest average time served in prison in 2020, with an average of 2.8 years. Other races people experienced shorter prison stays, averaging 1.2 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: By offense type, disparities were observed in time served by race and ethnicity. For rape or sexual assault offenses, Hispanic people had 12 more years on average compared to people of Other* races, who had the shortest time served for these offenses. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Gender In progress. Same type of graph as race and ethnicity tab. :::
